title: Speedboat Hire in Marbella: 2026 Costs, Tips and Top Boats
html:
For a day on the Costa del Sol, speedboat hire in Marbella offers freedom to explore the Mediterranean from Puerto Banús. Prices typically start around €350 for a half-day self-drive and can reach €1,200 for a full-day crewed experience. BoatHire24 connects you with verified boats in this premier yachting hub.

How much does it cost to rent a speedboat in Marbella?

Expect to pay between €350 and €1,500 for a half-day speedboat hire, depending on model and crew. Smaller open boats from Axopar or Zodiac cost less, while larger craft such as the Prestige 500 push the price higher. You can compare verified boat listings through yacht charter Marbella on BoatHire24 to see exact seasonal rates.

Where to set off in Marbella

Most hires depart from Puerto Banús, the glamorous marina lined with designer boutiques and charter desks. The historic Puerto Deportivo de Marbella offers a quieter alternative within walking distance of the old town. To the west, the dunes of Cabopino and the smaller marina of Calahonda provide access to the open coast. Eastwards, the mirador at Elviria and the rocky coves near Bahía de Marbella give you a different view of the Sierra Blanca.

Which boat to rent

  1. Open speedboat, ideal for a spontaneous half-day, carrying up to four guests from €350.
  2. Weekender cruiser, a 7-to-9-metre cabin boat for a day trip to Cabo de San Antonio, six guests from €600.
  3. Bowrider, a sharp, family-friendly runabout perfect for towing inflatables, five guests from €450.
  4. Luxury speedboat, the Prestige 500 with flybridge and galley, for eight guests from €1,200; see the Prestige 500 listing for availability.
  5. RIB, a rigid inflatable for snorkelling and coastal hops, six guests from €400.

What do the different price bands include?

  • Shared tour on a 10-metre Okura: €95 per person, four-hour cruise west to Cabopino
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): €350 self-drive, no fuel, up to 20 nautical miles range
  • Catamaran full-day (Lagoon 42): €800 for up to 10 guests, includes crew and paddleboards
  • Full-day crewed speedboat (Prestige 500): €1,200 for eight, includes skipper, fuel and snorkel gear

Do you need a licence to hire a boat in Marbella in 2026?

In Spain, no licence is required for boats under 6 metres and 15 hp, but larger speedboats require a PNB licence or equivalent. Operators on BoatHire24 confirm exact requirements for each vessel. If you lack a licence, a skipper can be added for around €200 per day.

Tip: pick the boat up at 10am from Puerto Banús to catch the morning calm; the south-east levante wind often picks up after lunch, so you can have a sheltered swim in the bay of Elviria before noon.

At a glance

  • Price band: €350, €1,200 per hire
  • Peak months: June, July, August and September
  • Trip length: 4, 8 hours
  • Capacity: 4, 8 passengers
  • Currency: EUR
  • Licence check: varies; skippered option always available

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can I rent a speedboat without a skipper in Marbella?

You can if the boat is under 6m and low-horsepower, but most popular speedboats require a Spanish PNB licence or an ICC; the operator will confirm before you book.

What is the best month to rent a speedboat in Marbella?

May and June offer warm calm seas and thinner crowds, while September still reaches 26°C water temperature in the bay of Marbella.

How far can I go on a half-day speedboat hire from Marbella?

With a four-hour rental you can easily reach the cliffs of Cabo de San Antonio (10 nm) or cross to the coast of San Roque, about 15 nm.

What is the cancellation policy for a boat booking in Marbella?

BoatHire24's standard terms allow free cancellation up to 24 hours before departure, but check the specific listing for any operator variations.

BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Marbella or list your own. Whether you are chartering the Prestige 500 or a simple open speedboat, the platform makes it easy to compare verified options. Owners can list your boat and reach thousands of holidaymakers planning their 2026 trip to the Costa del Sol.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
